entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., a legume crop susceptible to seed-borne viruses

entities.name: PLANT VIRUSES
entities.description: Viruses that infect plants and can be transmitted via various biotic and abiotic agents

entities.name: TRANSMISSION
entities.description: The process by which plant viruses are spread from one host to another

entities.name: EPIDEMIOLOGY
entities.description: The study of the distribution and determinants of plant diseases in populations

entities.name: BIOTIC AGENTS
entities.description: Living organisms that act as vectors for the transmission of plant viruses

entities.name: INSECTS
entities.description: Biotic agents that transmit plant viruses

entities.name: NEMATODES
entities.description: Biotic agents that transmit plant viruses

entities.name: FUNGI
entities.description: Biotic agents that transmit plant viruses

entities.name: SEEDS
entities.description: Plant reproductive structures that can transmit viruses to offspring

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: A group of plants, including groundnut, where seed transmission of viruses is common

entities.name: POLLEN
entities.description: Plant male gametophytes that can serve as a medium for virus transmission

entities.name: VEGETATIVE PROPAGATION
entities.description: A method of asexual plant reproduction that can transmit viruses

entities.name: SEED TRANSMISSION
entities.description: The ability of a virus to be transmitted from the parent plant to the offspring via seeds

entities.name: DETECTION
entities.description: The process of identifying the presence of seed-borne viruses in a crop
